[[ja:%E3%81%95%E3%81%8D%E3%81%9F%E3%81%BE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

[[ja:%E3%81%95%E3%81%8D%E3%81%9F%E3%81%BE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92]][[Category:kk]][[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]

'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.

== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

revision.text:
{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}

== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]

{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]

revision.text:
{{公園 | 名称 = さきたま古墳公園 | 英語名称 = | 画像 = [[ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | 画像キャプション = 芝生広場 | 国 = {{JPN}} | 都市 = [[埼玉県]][[行田市]]大字佐間、渡柳、樋上、堤根、埼玉 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 42 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 28 |経度秒 = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | 分類 = [[都市公園]]（広域公園） | 面積 = 37.4ha（計画：97.0ha） | 前身 = さきたま風土記の丘 | 開園 = [[1976年]]（昭和51年）[[4月1日]] | 運営者 = 埼玉県 | 年来園者数 = | 現況 = | 設備・遊具 = | 駐車場 = あり | バリアフリー = | 建築家と技術者 = | 告示 = | 事務所 = | 事務所所在地 = | 電話番号 = | 備考 = | 公式サイト = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html 埼玉県のサイト] }} '''さきたま古墳公園'''（さきたまこふんこうえん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]に位置する埼玉県営の[[都市公園]]（広域公園）である<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html 埼玉県公園整備に関するデータ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=2014年7月14日 }}</ref>。
== 概要 == [[行田市]]の[[市街地]]より南東へ約1[[キロメートル|km]]にある[[埼玉古墳群]]は、[[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）8月に、国により[[史跡]]（[[2020年]]3月に[[特別史跡]]に昇格）として指定を受け、[[1967年]]（昭和42年）に文化庁の「[[風土記の丘]]設置構想」に基づく史跡公園として、全国で2番目の風土記の丘となる 「'''さきたま風土記の丘'''」として整備を開始した。[[丸墓山古墳]]は[[日本一の一覧|日本一]]の規模の[[円墳]]と言われており、[[稲荷山古墳 (行田市)|稲荷山古墳]]より発見された[[稲荷山古墳出土鉄剣|金錯銘鉄剣]]115文字の銘文が持つ歴史上の意義から、世間の注目を集めた。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）4月より、これら古墳群との調和の取れた[[観賞]]・[[保護]]、及び[[散策]]を目的とした公園の整備が、埼玉県によって進められている。今後も予定地の収用や古墳の復元を進め、順次供用を予定している。2007年（平成19年）2月に[[日本の歴史公園100選]]に選定された。
== 施設・設備 == [[ファイル:Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|春の丸墓山古墳]] [[ファイル:Gyoda Futagoyama Tumulus In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|秋の二子山古墳]] * [[埼玉古墳群]] * [[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館]] * [[将軍山古墳]]展示館（埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館 分館） * はにわの館（実際に埴輪を作ることができる） * 移築古民家 * 芝生広場 * 古代の草原 * 古代の森 * さきたま広場 * 遊戯広場 * 薬用植物園 * 梅園 * スイレン池 * ハス池（[[行田蓮]]） * 休憩舎 * 四阿（あずまや） * [[トイレ]] * [[駐車場]]（普通車303台） <gallery> ファイル:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|[[奥の山古墳]]へ続く園路 ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|はにわの館 ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|移築古民家（遠藤家） ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|移築古民家（山崎家） </gallery>
== さきたま火祭り == [[ファイル:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|さきたま火祭り]]毎年[[5月4日]]にさきたま古墳公園内の芝生広場で行われる。「火祭り」という名は古事記に由来するものである。
古事記によれば、[[ニニギ|ニニギの命]]が[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|コノハナサクヤ姫]]と婚姻。ニニギノミコトに一夜の交わりで身ごもったのを疑われたコノハナサクヤ姫は、疑いを晴らすために産屋に火を放ち、その中で[[ホデリ]]（海幸彦）と[[ホオリ]]（山幸彦）を生んだとある。
日中は郷土芸能や物産展、[[フリーマーケット]]等のイベントが行われる。日没後、古代衣装を身につけた人々が[[たいまつ|松明]]を掲げ、産屋炎上、丸墓山古墳と稲荷山古墳の御神火下りが行われる。
[[2000年]]（平成12年）[[5月5日]]には、「21世紀に残したい・埼玉ふるさと自慢100選」に指定された。 <gallery> ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|産屋 ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|産屋炎上 ファイル:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|2018年のさきたま火祭り ファイル:Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|丸墓山の御神火下り </gallery>
== 所在地 == [[ファイル:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|公園入口]] * [[埼玉県]][[行田市]]大字佐間、渡柳、樋上、堤根、埼玉（さきたま）地内
== 交通アクセス == * [[高崎線]][[北鴻巣駅]]より、さきたま緑道で[[徒歩所要時間|徒歩]]約60分（4.5km）。 ** [[2009年]]7月4日から1年間の予定で「さきたまシャトルバス」の実証運行が開始され、行田市商工会議所「さきたまシャトルバス運行実証実験協議会」と[[協同バス]]が運行し、専用車両も用意された<ref>『[[読売新聞]]』2009年6月30日付。実証運行は行田市商工会議所「さきたまシャトルバス運行実証実験協議会」によって行われたが、協同バスの社長が行田市商工会議所会長を務めていたことも記されている。</ref>。北鴻巣駅とさきたま古墳公園を所要時間15分で結び、1時間に1便運行していたが、利用がふるわなかったのか、翌[[2010年]]7月19日をもって運行終了した<ref>[https://www.gyoda-cci.or.jp/bus/ さきたまシャトルバス終了のお知らせ さきたまシャトルバスは2010年7月19日をもって運行を終了いたしました。] さきたまシャトルバス運行実証実験協議会（行田商工会議所公式サイト内）</ref>。 * 高崎線[[吹上駅 (埼玉県)|吹上駅]]より、[[朝日自動車|朝日バス]]佐間経由行田車庫行にて「産業道路」下車、徒歩約15分。 * [[高崎線]][[行田駅]]より、[[行田市内循環バス]]「観光拠点循環」乗車（協同バスが運行、所要時間約25分）。「埼玉古墳公園前」下車、徒歩2分。 * [[秩父鉄道]][[行田市駅]]より、徒歩35分（2.7km）。 * [[東北自動車道]][[羽生インターチェンジ|羽生IC]]より北西へ約12km。
== 周辺 == [[ファイル:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|在りし日の「はにわ処 さかもと商店」]] * <del>[http://homepage2.nifty.com/haniwadokoro/ はにわ処 さかもと商店]</del> - みやげ物店。火災により焼失し現存しない。 * 海東山天祥寺<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm 海東山天祥寺]</ref> * [[前玉神社]]（さきたまじんじゃ）<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ 前玉神社]</ref> - [[浅間塚古墳]]がある。 * 古墳亭 - うどん店 * [[さきたま緑道]] - [[武蔵水路]]沿いに設けられた[[緑道]]。 * [[古代蓮の里]]までを結ぶ2kmほどの遊歩道が[[忍川#旧忍川|旧忍川]]沿いに整備されている * [[埼玉スバル#日本航空館|日本航空館]] - 埼玉スバルが運営する[[企業博物館]]。 {{-}}
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[埼玉古墳群]] * [[埼玉県#名称]] - 地名「埼玉」の由来について * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の観光地]] * [[日本の都市公園100選]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html さきたま古墳公園] - 埼玉県ホームページ * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ さきたま史跡の博物館] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ はにわの館] - 財団法人行田市産業・文化・スポーツいきいき財団 * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ 「さきたま古墳公園（行田市）」] - サイタマどうが（埼玉県公式アカウント） * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DPRxFl-hDkc 「さきたま古墳公園【埼玉県公式観光動画】」] - 埼玉県公式観光サイト ちょこたび埼玉MOVIE（ちょこたび埼玉 埼玉県産業労働部観光課アカウント）
{{埼玉県立公園}} {{日本の都市公園100選}} {{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}}
{{デフォルトソート:さきたまこふんこうえん}} [[Category:埼玉県の公園]] [[Category:行田市の地理]] [[Category:1976年開業の施設]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[さきたま古墳公園]] to [[Sakitama Fudoki Hill]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

[[sn:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]] [[ja:%E3%81%95%E3%81%8D%E3%81%9F%E3%81%BE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92]] [[en:Sakitama Fudoki Hill]]

{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.

== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

revision.text:
{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]

revision.text:
{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}
