{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}

== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**

== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.

== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

revision.text:
{{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224997]] Wikidata name: Sakitama Kofun Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224997]] Wikidata name: Sakitama Kofun Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:さきたま古墳公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224997]] Wikidata name: Sakitama Kofun Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:さきたま古墳公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224997]] Wikidata name: Sakitama Kofun Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|さきたま古墳公園|version=99260101|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:さきたま古墳公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224997]] Wikidata name: Sakitama Kofun Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|さきたま古墳公園|version=99260101|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:さきたま古墳公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:さきたま古墳公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:さきたま古墳公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224997]] Wikidata name: Sakitama Kofun Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox park | name = Sakitama Kofun Park | image = [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Lawn Field 1.JPG|300px]] | caption = Lawn field | country = {{JPN}} | city = [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]] | latitude = 36 | lat_minutes = 07 | lat_seconds = 42 | longitude = 139 | long_minutes = 28 | long_seconds = 46 | map = Japan Saitama | type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Regional park) | area = 37.4ha (planned: 97.0ha) | former_name = Sakitama Fudoki-no-Oka | created = [[:en:April 1|April 1]], [[:en:1976|1976]] | operator = Saitama Prefecture | facilities = | parking = Available | website = [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091203-13.html Saitama Prefectural Website] }}
'''Sakitama Kofun Park''' (さきたま古墳公園, *Sakitama Kofun Kōen*) is a [[:en:regional park|regional park]] located in [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda, Saitama]], operated by [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]<ref>[http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html Data on Saitama Prefecture Park Development] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714140744/http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/page/910-20091204-118.html |date=July 14, 2014 }}</ref>.
== Overview == Located about **1 km southeast** of Gyōda’s city center, the **Saitama Kofun Cluster** was designated as a [[Historic Site of Japan]] in **1938** and later upgraded to a **Special Historic Site** in **March 2020**. In **1967**, it was selected as part of the **"Fudoki-no-Oka" Project** initiated by Japan’s Agency for Cultural Affairs, making it the second site in the country to be developed as a **historical park**.
The **Maruhakayama Kofun**, recognized as the **largest round Kofun in Japan**, and the **Inariyama Kofun**, where the **famous gold-inlaid iron sword** (*Kin-saku-mei Tekken*) with 115 inscribed characters was excavated, have drawn significant historical interest. Since **April 1976**, Saitama Prefecture has been developing the park to **harmonize preservation, sightseeing, and leisure activities**. The project continues with the **planned acquisition of additional land** and **restoration of kofun structures**. In **2007**, the park was selected as one of the **"100 Historical Parks of Japan"**.
== Facilities == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Maruhakayama Kofun in spring]] [[File:Gyoda Futagoyama Kofun In Autumn 1.JPG|thumb|230px|right|Futagoyama Kofun in autumn]] * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Saitama Prefectural Sakitama Archaeological Museum]] * **Shogunyama Kofun Exhibition Hall** (a branch of the museum) * **Haniwa-no-Kan** (hands-on haniwa pottery workshop) * **Relocated historic houses** * **Lawn Field** * **Ancient Grassland** * **Ancient Forest** * **Sakitama Plaza** * **Children's Play Area** * **Medicinal Plant Garden** * **Plum Garden** * **Water Lily Pond** * **Lotus Pond** (*Gyōda Lotus*) * **Resting Pavilion** * **Azumaya (Japanese gazebo)** * **Restrooms** * **Parking lot (303 spaces)**
<gallery> File:Sakitama-kofun-park2011.jpg|Path leading to [[Okunoyama Kofun]] File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Haniwa House 1.JPG|Haniwa-no-Kan File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Endo House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Endo Residence) File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Yamazaki House 1.JPG|Relocated historic house (Yamazaki Residence) </gallery>
== Sakitama Fire Festival == [[File:Saitamafirefestival2011.jpg|thumb|230px|right|Sakitama Fire Festival]]
Held annually on **May 4**, the **Sakitama Fire Festival** takes place on the park’s **Lawn Field**. The festival is inspired by the **Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)**.
According to the Kojiki, **Ninigi-no-Mikoto** married **Konohanasakuya-hime**, who became pregnant in a single night. Doubting her fidelity, Ninigi questioned the legitimacy of their children. To prove her innocence, Konohanasakuya-hime set fire to the birthing hut and gave birth inside the flames to **Hoderi (Umisachihiko)** and **Hoori (Yamasachihiko)**.
During the **daytime**, visitors can enjoy **traditional folk performances, local crafts, and a flea market**. After sunset, people dressed in **ancient attire** light **torches**, followed by a **symbolic fire ritual** featuring the **burning of the birthing hut** and a **sacred fire procession** from **Maruhakayama Kofun** and **Inariyama Kofun**.
In **2000**, the festival was selected as one of **"Saitama’s 100 Treasured Cultural Traditions for the 21st Century"**.
<gallery> File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 2.JPG|Birthing hut File:Gyoda Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Burning of the birthing hut File:Sakitama fire festival 2018 5 4.webm|Sakitama Fire Festival 2018 File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Sakitama Fire Festival 1.JPG|Sacred fire descending from Maruhakayama Kofun </gallery>
== Location == [[File:Gyoda Sakitama Kofun Park Entrance 1.JPG|thumb|right|230px|Park entrance]] * **Address**: Sama, Wataruya, Hinoe, Tsutsumine, Sakitama, **Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture**
== Access == * **Rail** ** **60 min walk (4.5 km)** from **Kitakōnosu Station** (Takasaki Line) via the **Sakitama Greenway**. ** **15 min bus ride** from **Kitakōnosu Station** via the experimental **Sakitama Shuttle Bus** (2009-2010, now discontinued). ** **15 min walk** from **Sangyō-dōro Bus Stop**, serviced by **Asahi Bus from Fukaya Station**. ** **2 min walk** from **Saitama Kofun Park Bus Stop**, served by **Gyōda City Circulation Bus (Tourist Loop Route)**. ** **35 min walk (2.7 km)** from **Gyōda-shi Station** (Chichibu Railway).
* **Car** ** **12 km northwest** from **Hanyū IC (Tohoku Expressway)**.
== Nearby Attractions == [[File:Gyoda Haniwadokoro Sakamoto Store 1.JPG|thumb|right|Former "Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store" (destroyed by fire)]] * **<del>Haniwa-dokoro Sakamoto Store</del>** – A souvenir shop (destroyed by fire, no longer in existence). * **Kaitōzan Tenshō-ji Temple**<ref>[http://tensyouji.sakura.ne.jp/index.htm Tenshō-ji Temple]</ref>. * **[[Sakitama Shrine]]** – Home to the **Asama-zuka Kofun**<ref>[http://sakitama-jinja.com/ Sakitama Shrine]</ref>. * **Kofun-tei** – A local **udon restaurant**. * **Sakitama Greenway** – A scenic pathway along the **Musashi Waterway**. * **2 km walking trail** linking the park to **Kodai Hasu-no-Sato (Ancient Lotus Village)**. * **Japan Aviation Museum** – Operated by **Saitama Subaru**.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Saitama Kofun Cluster]] * [[Origin of the Name "Saitama"]] * [[List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Tourist Attractions in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[100 Selected Urban Parks of Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/a0301/kodomo/901-20100219-350776.html Sakitama Kofun Park] (Saitama Prefecture) * [http://www.sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/ Sakitama Archaeological Museum] * [http://www.ikiiki-zaidan.or.jp/haniwa/ Haniwa-no-Kan] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GfTSnooNzjQ Official YouTube Video (Saitama Prefecture)]
{{Kofun navbar}}
